region_of_origin: Indian subcontinent; widely consumed in Singapore

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general naan bread entry (FOODON_03315671); no specific keema-stuffed variant exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific keema naan entry. A000E (Bread and similar products) is the closest defensible category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code exists for keema naan; omitted to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard piece of keema naan as served in a Singaporean Indian restaurant weighs approximately 150g.

nutrient_source.title: Keema Naan — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Keema Naan (cr_id G00010858). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat is not reported by HPB for this entry and has been estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for naan bread with meat filling (closest analogous entry); see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for keema naan in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 71 is estimated from the closest analogous entry: white wheat flour naan bread (GI ~71, University of Sydney GI Database). The minced meat filling moderates glycaemic response marginally but the refined wheat flour base dominates. GL per serving calculated as: (71 × 75.23 × 1.50) / 100 = 80.1 for a 150g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 632mg per 100g — exceeds the 600mg/100g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should strictly limit keema naan intake; sodium at 632mg/100g means a single 150g serving delivers approximately 948mg sodium, representing over 40% of the 2300mg daily limit, and should be avoided alongside other salty dishes or condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High carbohydrate load (75.2g/100g) from refined wheat flour with a high estimated GI of 71, producing a very high glycaemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat keema naan as a high-GI, high-GL food; a standard 150g serving delivers approximately 113g of carbohydrate with a glycaemic load of ~80, requiring careful insulin or medication adjustment and strict portion control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (632mg/100g) combined with saturated fat from minced mutton and ghee, and refined carbohydrate driving atherogenic postprandial lipid response
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit keema naan due to its high sodium content, saturated fat from mutton keema and ghee, and refined carbohydrate load that collectively elevate blood pressure, LDL cholesterol, and triglyceride risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat (mutton keema) is rich in arachidonic acid, a direct precursor to pro-inflammatory eicosanoids; refined flour adds glycaemic inflammatory load
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or strictly limit keema naan as the mutton filling provides arachidonic acid that amplifies inflammatory pathways, while the refined wheat flour base contributes to high glycaemic load and systemic inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Keema Naan is a leavened Indian flatbread made from refined wheat flour dough that is stuffed with spiced minced meat — traditionally mutton (lamb) or beef — and baked in a tandoor (clay oven) at high heat. The bread is soft, slightly charred on the surface, and chewy in texture, with a savoury, spiced meat filling that is seasoned with onion, green chilli, ginger, garlic, cumin, coriander, and garam masala. It is commonly finished with a brushing of ghee or butter, adding richness and gloss. In Singapore, keema naan is a staple offering at North Indian and Pakistani restaurants, often served as a main bread accompaniment to curries, dals, or raita. Nutritionally, keema naan is energy-dense at 412 kcal per 100g, with a high carbohydrate content of 75.2g/100g from refined wheat flour, moderate protein at 9.85g/100g from the meat filling, and moderate fat at 8.25g/100g. Sodium is elevated at 632mg/100g due to seasoning in the keema filling. The estimated glycaemic index is high (~71), making this a high-glycaemic-load food unsuitable for uncontrolled Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension. The mutton filling contributes arachidonic acid, which is relevant for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis or inflammatory conditions. Keema naan is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans, contains gluten and dairy allergens, and is generally considered halal when prepared with mutton.
common_user_queries: How many calories in keema naan?
Is keema naan high in sodium?
What is keema naan made of?
Is keema naan suitable for hypertension?
Keema naan vs plain naan calories

confidence_reason: Core macronutrients and micronutrients sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry (cr_id G00010858, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous naan-with-meat entry. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous white wheat naan entry (GI ~71). All primary nutrient data from an approved primary regional source.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (2.8g/100g) is an analogous estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for naan bread with meat filling, as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this entry. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific keema naan preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 3. cholesterol_mg is reported as 0.0 by HPB; this is confirmed zero per the HPB source record. 4. GI of 71 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database white wheat naan entry; no direct keema naan GI measurement was found in approved sources. 5. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as (71 × 75.23 × 1.50) / 100 = 80.1 for a standard 150g serving. 6. Halal status assumed based on mutton filling; preparation method at individual restaurants may vary.
